description: Wave 1 - M08 Media Gateway: Implement MediaMTX configuration and stream proxy management for up to 4 camera streams. Branch: feat/m08-media-gateway
steps: Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/interface.go defining MediaProxy interface: AddStream, RemoveStream, GetStreamStatus, Health
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/types.go with StreamConfig, StreamStatus, MediaHealth structs
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/config.go with MediaMTX configuration generation (RTSP source pull, WebRTC/HLS output, codec passthrough, no transcoding, no recording)
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/config_test.go testing config generation for 1-4 cameras
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/manager.go implementing stream lifecycle management with on-demand connection
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/manager_test.go testing: add/remove stream, health check, reconnection logic
Create services/gateway-server/internal/adapters/media/simulator/simulator.go mock MediaProxy for testing without MediaMTX
Create infra/edge/mediamtx/mediamtx.example.yml with production-like MediaMTX config template (4 stream paths, WebRTC enabled, no recording)
Ensure no transcoding or recording logic exists
Ensure camera credentials are referenced from encrypted local config only (no hardcoded credentials)
Ensure streams reconnect independently (one camera failure does not affect others)
Document performance budget: 4 streams within 1GB memory target
Run make build and make test
acceptance_criteria: MediaProxy interface supports up to 4 simultaneous streams
Configuration generates valid MediaMTX compatible config
No transcoding or recording logic present
Streams are independent (one failure does not block others)
